content.description:
Type for kernel subsystem modules. These are the foundational code items that compose the [kernel](item://c0c0c0c0-0001-0000-0000-000000000000)—the minimal runtime that everything else builds upon.

## Kernel Modules

| Module | Responsibility |
|--------|---------------|
| [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) | Orchestration, boot sequence, events, REPL API |
| [kernel:storage](item://33333333-2222-0000-0000-000000000000) | IndexedDB wrapper, type chain validation |
| [kernel:module-system](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000) | Code loading, caching, api.require() |
| [kernel:rendering](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000) | View dispatch, renderer API, instance registry |
| [kernel:safe-mode](item://33333333-7777-0000-0000-000000000000) | Recovery interface |
| [kernel:styles](item://33333333-8888-0000-0000-000000000000) | Base CSS styles |

## Why Kernel Modules Are Items

- **Inspectable**: View source code through Hobson's UI
- **Linkable**: Documentation can reference specific functions via `item://id#code?region=name`
- **Modifiable**: (Carefully) customize kernel behavior
- **Self-describing**: Each module documents itself

## Caution

Editing kernel modules can break the system. Use [safe mode](item://c0c0c0c0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) to recover if needed.

## See Also

- [concept:kernel](item://c0c0c0c0-0001-0000-0000-000000000000) — What the kernel is
- [kernel:code](item://22222222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Base type for all code items
